content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian.Utkarsh is forced to play another game with Ashish.

In this game there are NNN piles, ithi^{th}ith pile contains AiA_iAi​ stones. **Utkarsh moves first**.

In his turn a player can choose any pile with non-zero stones and remove exactly one stone from that pile. The game ends when there is exactly one pile containing non-zero stones and the player who made the last move wins the game.

Now you are given an array BBB of length NNN. Utkarsh wants to know for how many arrays A such that 1≤Ai≤Bi1 \leq A_i \leq B_i1≤Ai​≤Bi​ for every iii, he will win the game assuming that both players play optimally.

Since the answer can be large output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each test case consists of an integer NNN.
Second line consists of NNN space separated integers denoting the array BBB.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the number of games Utkarsh wins modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, Utkarsh wins the game for following arrays:

[1,1][1,1][1,1], [1,2][1,2][1,2], [1,3][1,3][1,3], [2,1][2,1][2,1], [3,1][3,1][3,1], [2,3][2,3][2,3], [3,2][3,2][3,2].
So there are total 777 possibilities.
